summary:
The presentation fits acute pseudomembraneous candidiasis due to characteristic painless, removable white plaques leaving red mucosal surfaces without significant inflammation or pain—common in high-risk individuals. Azoles (option C) inhibit 14-alpha-demethylase, blocking ergosterol synthesis essential for fungal membrane stability—the correct pharmacologic mechanism underlying treatment choice.

Okay, I need to summarize Agent1’s reasoning based strictly on its content within two concise sentences focusing only on core clinical aspects:

First sentence should identify what condition fits the signs/symptoms clearly—from the history and physical findings (white, brush-off films causing erythema, absence of pain/vesicular lesions).

Second sentence must state why that specific diagnosis makes sense over alternatives—and briefly mention how drug mechanisms tie into answering the implied query about therapy rationale.

I'll avoid any tangential details unrelated directly to diagnosing or explaining management logic.
